sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: ὡς οὐ καθέξω τειχέων ἔσω στρατόν .
sections.translation: I will not keep my army within the walls .

sections.label: Section 721
sections.original: καὶ μὴν τὸ νικᾶν ἐστι πᾶν εὐβουλία .
sections.translation: And yet victory is entirely a matter of good counsel .

sections.label: Section 722
sections.original: βούλῃ τράπωμαι δῆθʼ ὁδοὺς ἄλλας τινάς ;
sections.translation: Do you then want me to turn to some other way ?

sections.label: Section 723
sections.original: πάσας γε , πρὶν κίνδυνον εἰς ἅπαξ μολεῖν .
sections.translation: Yes , to every one , before running the risk once for all .

sections.label: Section 724
sections.original: εἰ νυκτὸς αὐτοῖς προσβάλοιμεν ἐκ λόχου ;
sections.translation: Suppose we fall on them by night from ambush ?

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: εἴπερ σφαλείς γε δεῦρο σωθήσῃ πάλιν .
sections.translation: Yes , if in the event of defeat you can return safely here .

sections.label: Section 726
sections.original: ἴσον φέρει νύξ , τοῖς δὲ τολμῶσιν πλέον .
sections.translation: Night equalizes risks , though it rather favors daring .

sections.label: Section 727
sections.original: ἐνδυστυχῆσαι δεινὸν εὐφρόνης κνέφας .
sections.translation: The darkness of night is a terrible time to suffer disaster .

sections.label: Section 728
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἀμφὶ δεῖπνον οὖσι προσβάλω δόρυ ;
sections.translation: Well , shall I attack them as they sit at dinner ?

sections.label: Section 729
sections.original: ἔκπληξις ἂν γένοιτο · νικῆσαι δὲ δεῖ .
sections.translation: That might cause them fright , but victory is what we need .

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: βαθύς γέ τοι Διρκαῖος ἀναχωρεῖν πόρος .
sections.translation: Dirce ’ s ford is certainly deep enough to prevent their retreat .

sections.label: Section 731
sections.original: ἅπαν κάκιον τοῦ φυλάσσεσθαι καλῶς .
sections.translation: No plan so good as to keep well guarded .

sections.label: Section 732
sections.original: τί δʼ , εἰ καθιππεύσαιμεν Ἀργείων στρατόν ;
sections.translation: What if we ride out against the army of Argos ?

sections.label: Section 733
sections.original: κἀκεῖ πέφρακται λαὸς ἅρμασιν πέριξ .
sections.translation: Their troops too are fenced all round with chariots .

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: τί δῆτα δράσω ; πολεμίοισι δῶ πόλιν ;
sections.translation: What shall I do , then ? Am I to surrender the city to the enemy ?

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: μὴ δῆτα · βουλεύου δʼ , ἐπείπερ εἶ σοφός .
sections.translation: No indeed ! But out of your wisdom form some plan .

sections.label: Section 736
sections.original: τίς οὖν πρόνοια γίγνεται σοφωτέρα ;
sections.translation: What forethought is wiser than mine ?

sections.label: Section 737
sections.original: ἕπτʼ ἄνδρας αὐτοῖς φασιν , ὡς ἤκουσʼ ἐγώ —
sections.translation: They have seven men , I hear —

sections.label: Section 738
sections.original: τί προστετάχθαι δρᾶν ; τὸ γὰρ σθένος βραχύ .
sections.translation: What is their appointed task ? their might is small .

sections.label: Section 739
sections.original: λόχων ἀνάσσειν ἑπτὰ προσκεῖσθαι πύλαις .
sections.translation: . . . To attack the seven gates .

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: τί δῆτα δρῶμεν ; ἀπορίαν γὰρ οὐ μενῶ .
sections.translation: What are we to do then ? I will not wait till every chance is gone .

sections.label: Section 741
sections.original: ἕπτʼ ἄνδρας αὐτοῖς καὶ σὺ πρὸς πύλαις ἑλοῦ .
sections.translation: You also choose seven men to set against them at the gates .

sections.label: Section 742
sections.original: λόχων ἀνάσσειν ἢ μονοστόλου δορός ;
sections.translation: To lead our companies , or to fight single-handed ?

sections.label: Section 743
sections.original: λόχων , προκρίνας οἵπερ ἀλκιμώτατοι .
sections.translation: To lead ; choose the very bravest ones .

sections.label: Section 744
sections.original: ξυνῆκʼ · ἀμύνειν τειχέων προσαμβάσεις .
sections.translation: I understand ; to repel attempts at scaling our walls .

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: καὶ ξυστρατήγους γʼ · εἷς δʼ ἀνὴρ οὐ πάνθʼ ὁρᾷ .
sections.translation: With others to share the command , for one man doesn ’ t see everything .

sections.label: Section 746
sections.original: θάρσει προκρίνας ἢ φρενῶν εὐβουλίᾳ ;
sections.translation: Selecting them for courage or thoughtful prudence ?

sections.label: Section 747
sections.original: ἀμφότερον · ἀπολειφθὲν γὰρ οὐδὲν θάτερον .
sections.translation: Both ; for one is nothing without the other .

sections.label: Section 748
sections.original: ἔσται τάδʼ · ἐλθὼν ἑπτάπυργον ἐς πόλιν
sections.translation: It shall be done ; I will go to our seven towers and post captains at the gates , as you say ,

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: ἴσους ἴσοισι πολεμίοισιν ἀντιθείς .
sections.translation: pitting them man for man against the enemy . To tell each one ’ s name is a great waste of time , when the enemy are camped beneath our very walls . But I will go , that my hands may no longer hang idle . And may I find my brother face to face ,

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: καὶ ξυσταθέντα διὰ μάχης ἑλεῖν δορί .
sections.translation: meet him in battle and kill him with my spear and kill him , for coming to waste my country ! But if I suffer any misfortune , you must see to the marriage between Antigone , my sister and Haemon , your son ; and now , as I take my leave ,

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: τὴν πρόσθε ποιῶ νῦν ἐπʼ ἐξόδοις ἐμαῖς .
sections.translation: I ratify their previous betrothal . You are my mother ’ s brother , no need to speak at length . Take care of her as she deserves , both for your own sake and mine . As for my father , he has been guilty of folly against himself in putting out his eyes ; I have small praise for him ;

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: ἡμᾶς δʼ ἀραῖσιν , ἢν τύχῃ , κατακτενεῖ .
sections.translation: by his curses it may be that he will slay us too .

sections.label: Section 766
sections.original: ἓν δʼ ἐστὶν ἡμῖν ἀργόν , εἴ τι θέσφατον
sections.translation: One thing we still have to do : ask Teiresias , the seer , if he has anything to say of heaven ’ s will . I will send your son Menoeceus , who bears your father ’ s name ,

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: λαβόντα πέμψω δεῦρο Τειρεσίαν , Κρέον ·
sections.translation: to fetch Teiresias here , Creon ; for he will readily converse with you , but I have before now so scorned his prophetic art to his face , that he has reasons to reproach me . This commandment , Creon , I lay upon the city and you :

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: ἤνπερ κρατήσῃ τἀμά , Πολυνείκους νέκυν
sections.translation: if my cause should prevail , never give Polyneices ’ corpse a grave in Theban soil , and let the one who buries him die , even if it is a friend . This I say to you ; and this to my servants Bring out my weapons and armor ,

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: ὡς εἰς ἀγῶνα τὸν προκείμενον δορὸς
sections.translation: so that I may start at once for the appointed combat , with justice to lead to victory . We will pray to Caution , the most useful goddess , to save our city . Exit Eteocles .

sections.label: Section 1.784
sections.original: ὦ πολύμοχθος Ἄρης , τί ποθʼ αἵματι
sections.translation: O Ares , god of much suffering ! Why , why are you possessed by a love of blood and

sections.label: Section 1.785
sections.original: καὶ θανάτῳ κατέχῃ Βρομίου παράμουσος ἑορταῖς ;
sections.translation: death , out of harmony with the festivals of Bromius ? Not for young girls crowned in the lovely dance do you toss your curls , singing to the flute ’ s breath a song to charm the dancers ’ feet ; no , with warriors clad in armor you inspire the Argive army with a lust

sections.label: Section 1.790
sections.original: αἵματι Θήβας
sections.translation: for Theban blood , leading your revels that are held without music . Nor do you rush with wild waving of the thyrsus , clad in fawnskin , but with chariots and horses you go to the waters of Ismenus , inspiring the Argives

sections.label: Section 1.795
sections.original: Σπαρτῶν γένναν ,
sections.translation: with hatred for the Spartans , arraying in bronze armor against these stone-built walls a band of warriors and their shields .

sections.label: Section 1.798a
sections.original: ἦ δεινά τις Ἔρις θεός , ἃ τάδε
sections.translation: Truly Strife is a goddess to fear , who devised these troubles for the princes of this land ,

sections.label: Section 1.800
sections.original: Λαβδακίδαις πολυμόχθοις .
sections.translation: for the much-suffering sons of Labdacus .

sections.label: Section 1.801
sections.original: ὦ ζαθέων πετάλων πολυθηρότα-
sections.translation: O snow-capped Cithaeron , dear to Artemis , holy vale of leaves , crowded with wild animals , would that you had never reared the one exposed to die , Oedipus , Jocasta ’ s child , when as a baby he was cast forth from his home ,

sections.label: Section 1.805
sections.original: χρυσοδέτοις περόναις ἐπίσαμον ·
sections.translation: marked with a golden brooch ; and would that the Sphinx , that winged maid , monster from the hills , had never come as a grief to our land with her inharmonious songs , she that once drew near our walls and snatched the sons of Cadmus away in her taloned feet to the untrodden light of heaven ,

sections.label: Section 1.810
sections.original: γένναν , ἃν ὁ κατὰ χθονὸς Ἅιδας
sections.translation: sent by Hades from hell to plague the men of Thebes ; once more unhappy strife is coming into bloom between the sons of Oedipus in home and city . For never can wrong be right ,

sections.label: Section 1.815
sections.original: οὐδʼ οἱ μὴ νόμιμοι
sections.translation: nor can there be good in unlawful children , their mother ’ s birth pangs , their father ’ s pollution ; she came to the bed of her son . . . .

sections.label: Section 818
sections.original: ἔτεκες , ὦ Γαῖʼ , ἔτεκές ποτε ,
sections.translation: O Earth , you once bore — as I heard , I heard the story told by foreigners once in my own home — you bore

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: τὰν ἀπὸ θηροτρόφου φοινικολόφοιο δράκοντος
sections.translation: a race which sprang of the teeth of a snake with blood-red crest , that fed on beasts , to be the glory and reproach of Thebes .

sections.label: Section 822
sections.original: Ἁρμονίας δέ ποτʼ εἰς ὑμεναίους
sections.translation: In days gone by the sons of heaven came to the wedding of Harmonia , and the walls and towers of Thebes rose to the sound of Amphion ’ s lyre ,

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: διδύμων ποταμῶν πόρον ἀμφὶ μέσον ,
sections.translation: in the midst between the double streams where Dirce waters the grass-green field before Ismenus ; and Io , our horned ancestress , was mother of the kings of Thebes ;

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: μυριάδας δʼ ἀγαθῶν ἑτέροις ἑτέ-
sections.translation: thus our city , through an endless succession of various blessings , has set herself upon the heights , crowned with the glory of war .

sections.label: Section 834
sections.original: ἡγοῦ πάροιθε , θύγατερ · ὡς τυφλῷ ποδὶ
sections.translation: led in by his daughter . Lead on , my daughter ; for you are an eye

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: ὀφθαλμὸς εἶ σύ , ναυβάταισιν ἄστρον ὥς ·
sections.translation: to my blind feet , as a star is to sailors ; lead my steps on to level ground ; then go before , so that I do not stumble , for your father has no strength ; keep safe for me in your maiden hand the auguries I took when I observed omens from birds ,

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: θάκοισιν ἐν ἱεροῖσιν , οὗ μαντεύομαι .
sections.translation: seated in my holy prophet ’ s chair . Tell me , Menoeceus , son of Creon , how much further toward the city is it , to your father ? For my knees grow weary , I have come a long way and can scarcely go on .

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: θάρσει · πέλας γάρ , Τειρεσία , φίλοισι σοῖς
sections.translation: Take heart , Teiresias , for you have reached your harbor and are near your friends ; take him by the hand , my child ; for just as every chariot has to wait for outside help to lighten it , so does the step of old age .

sections.label: Section 849
sections.original: εἶἑν , πάρεσμεν · τί με καλεῖς σπουδῇ , Κρέον ;
sections.translation: Enough ; I have arrived ; why , Creon , do you summon me so urgently ?

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: οὔπω λελήσμεθʼ · ἀλλὰ σύλλεξαι σθένος
sections.translation: I have not forgotten that ; but first collect your strength and regain your breath , shaking off the fatigue of your journey .

sections.label: Section 852
sections.original: κόπῳ παρεῖμαι γοῦν Ἐρεχθειδῶν ἄπο
sections.translation: I am indeed worn out , for I arrived here only yesterday from the court of the Erechtheidae ; they too were at war , fighting with Eumolpus .

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: οὗ καλλινίκους Κεκροπίδας ἔθηκʼ ἐγώ ·
sections.translation: I gave the victory to Cecrops ’ sons , and I received this golden crown , as you see , the first-fruits of the enemy ’ s spoils .
